original: Phosphates discovered on Enceladus, a moon of Saturn. Last of essential building blocks of life found there (others carbon, hydrogen, nitrogen, oxygen and sulfur) raising possibility of extraterrestrial life
Anne Frankbegins writing her diary, in Dutch, two days after her 13th birthday
The world's first stem-cell assisted vein transplant is undertaken by Swedish doctors on a 10-year-old girl
Denmark and Canada agree to split the arctic Hans Island, ending their 50-year "Whiskey War", where each country laid claim by buying whiskey on the island
First mammal made extinct by human-induced climate change announced - the Bramble Cay melomys from Torres Strait
Charles Babbageproposes a "difference engine" in a paper to the Royal Astronomical Society entitled "Note on the application of machinery to the computation of astronomical and mathematical tables"
Robert Bunseninvents the Bunsen burner
John Alcock and Arthur Brown leave Newfoundland in the first non-stop air crossing of the Atlantic
Cristiano Ronaldoremoves sponsors coke bottles from his press table at the European Championships, prompting the company share price to drop $4 billion
US Continental Congress adopts the Stars & Stripes flag, designed byFrancis Hopkinson, replacing the Grand Union flag
Petition to create the world's first "time-free zone" on Sommarøy Island, which experiences complete daylight for two months, is delivered to the Norwegian Parliament
‘Benchmark’ experiment by IBM quantum computer manages to simulate a magnetic material, working round previous problem of quantum noise
USSR launches Venera 10 for Venus landing
The European Space Research Organisation is established in Paris - later becoming the European Space Agency
Russian cosmonaut Valery Bykovsky, aboard Vostok 5, orbits earth 81 times in 5 days
Groundbreaking begins in Bloomington, Minnesota on world's largest mall - "The Mall of America"
Fossil bones found in Tam Pà Ling (Cave of the Monkeys), Laos, dated to 86,000 years, are oldest known examples of modern humans (Homo sapiens) in South-East Asia
Asafa Powell of Jamaica sets a new Men's 100 meters world record of 9.77 at the Athens Olympic Stadium
Leroy Burrell of USA sets 100m record (9.90) in NYC
Henry Ossian Flipperbecomes 1st African American to graduate from West Point Military Academy
E. Cuyler Hammond, American epidemiologist (first to link smoking with lung cancer), born in Baltimore, Maryland (d. 1986)
ExplorerIbn Battutabegins his travels, leaving his home in Tangiers to travel to Mecca (gone 24 years)
NASA launches Mariner 5 for a Venus flyby, from Cape Canaveral Air Force Station in Florida
Nat King Colerecords "The Christmas Song" (written by Mel Tormé and Bob Wells) for the first time
Burl Ives, American folk singer ("A Little Bitty Tear"; "Silver And Gold"), actor (Cat On A Hot Tin Roof), and snowman (Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer), born in Hunt City, Illinois (d. 1995)
Cliff Edwards, American vaudevillian and singer, also known as "Ukulele Ike" (Walt Disney's Pinocchio - "Jiminy Cricket"; "When You Wish Upon a Star"), born in Hannibal, Missouri (d. 1971)
Taeil [Moon Tae-il], Korean K-pop singer (NCT - "Make a Wish"), born in Seoul, South Korea
Daryl Sabara, American actor (Spy Kids films; Genrator Rex; The Polar Express), born in Torrance, California
K-pop groupBTSannounce they are taking a break to pursue individual projects
DrummerRingo Starrre-unites with the Beatles in Melbourne, Australia, after recovering from tonsillitis
"Jurassic World" is the first film to make $500 million worldwide in its opening weekend
"Cable Guy" starringJim Carreyis released
"Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves" opens directed by Kevin Reynolds and starringKevin CostnerandMorgan Freeman
CaptainWilliam Blighand his loyal men cast off from HMS Bounty reach Timor, after sailing 5,800 km in a 6-metre launch
MLB Angels'Nolan Ryanstrikes out 19 Boston Red Sox over 13 innings in a 15-inning 2-1 win for California at Anaheim Stadium
Oakland As'Reggie Jacksongets 10 RBIs to beat Red Sox 21-7
MLB Texas Rangers'Nolan Ryanbecomes 2nd pitcher to defeat all 26 teams
Stanley Cup Final, Madison Square Garden: New York Rangers beat Vancouver Canucks, 3-2 for a 4-3 series victory; Rangers end their record 54-year Championship drought
Miami beats St. Petersburg (Florida State League) 4-3 in 29 innings, the longest uninterrupted game in organized baseball
American boxerMax BaerKO's defending championPrimo Carneraof Italy in 11th round at Madison Square Garden for the lineal world heavyweight title
British runnerJim Petersruns world record marathon (2:20:42.2)
Rashid Khanmakes his Test debut for Afghanistan against India
1st player to hit for cycle (George Hall, Philadelphia Athletics)
Funeral forEmily Davisonincludes a procession of 6,000 suffragettes in London, England
Swiss women hold a national strike over the country's slow pace towards equality
Last part of the world's largest floating restaurant, the Jumbo Kingdom seafood restaurant, leaves Hong Kong after 46 years and sinks four days later near the Paracel Islands
Heatwave event begins across western US and Canadian states with Denver at 101 degrees and Helena 105 degrees
Atlantic, Iowa rainfall of 13.18 inches (33.5 cm) sets new state record
106°F, hottest temperature in San Francisco
42.0 cm rain falls on East St Louis, Illinois (state record)
First compulsory education law in America is passed by Massachusetts
PresidentEisenhowersigns order adding words "under God" to the Pledge
American children's literature writer and illustration Dorothy Lathrop wins 1st Caldecott Medal for Illustration for her work "Animals of the Bible"
Judith Kerr, British children's writer and illustrator (The Tiger Who Came to Tea, Mog books), born in Berlin, Germany (d. 2019)
Alonzo Church, American Mathematician whose work provided foundation for computer science, born in Washington, D.C. (d. 1995)
Jacob Ellehammer, Danish watchmaker and inventor (powered flight), born in Bakkebølle, Denmark (d. 1946)
Andrey Markov, Russian mathematician (Markov Chain), born in Ryazan, Russian Empire (d. 1922)
David Houston, Scottish-American inventor (Kodak camera), born in Auchterarder, Scotland (d. 1906)[1]
Margaret Bourke-White, American photographer, 1st female LIFE staff photographer, born in New York City (d. 1971)
Pat Summitt, American Basketball HOF coach (8 × NCAA Division I tournament; 18 × NCAA Regional—Final Four; University of Tennessee), born in Clarksville, Tennessee (d. 2016)
